diagrams.description: The mind map of microeconomics branches from the central idea into its fundamental building blocks: consumer theory (utility, budget, preferences), producer theory (production, costs, profit maximization), market structures (perfect competition, monopoly, oligopoly), factor markets (labour, capital), welfare economics (efficiency, equity, Pareto optimality), and market failures that call for corrective intervention (externalities, public goods, asymmetric information).
